description: National emergency number for police, fire, and ambulance services. Operated by the National Police Service. Coverage is strongest in major urban centers; rural response times vary significantly.

provenance.notes: Primary emergency number since independence; referenced in OSAC Country Security Report and multiple government sources

description: GSM standard emergency number. Routes to the same emergency services as 999. Works on any GSM mobile phone, including locked phones and phones without SIM cards where network coverage exists.

provenance.notes: ITU-mandated GSM emergency number; confirmed active in Kenya by multiple sources including OSAC and Global Rescue

description: Alternative emergency number, added for familiarity with the US emergency system. Routes to the same services as 999 and 112.

provenance.notes: Confirmed active by OSAC Kenya Country Security Report; less commonly referenced in Kenyan government materials than 999/112

description: Kenya Red Cross Society toll-free emergency and ambulance line. Available 24/7 nationwide. Also provides tele-counselling and mental health and psychosocial support (MHPSS) services.

provenance.notes: Toll-free from all Kenyan networks; alternate +254 703 037 000 confirmed via LifeLine International and KRCS official channels

description: National Gender-Based Violence toll-free helpline operated by Healthcare Assistance Kenya (HAK). Available 24/7. Provides immediate psychosocial support, referrals to shelters, legal aid, healthcare, and emergency rescue coordination.

provenance.notes: First and only national GBV helpline in Kenya. Reported 360% increase in calls during COVID-19 lockdowns (2020). Confirmed by UN Women and UNFPA Kenya.

description: National Child Helpline operated by Childline Kenya in partnership with the Department of Children Services (Government of Kenya). Toll-free, 24/7. Provides counselling, child therapy, family therapy, and referrals to child protection services across all 47 counties.

provenance.source: Childline Kenya / Department of Children Services
provenance.notes: Operational since 2008 on the 116 platform. Confirmed by UNICEF Kenya, Child Helpline International, and the official Government portal (childrenservices.go.ke).
